Attention: 
Legal Department
RE: Request fcr Advice Pursuant to Governrnent Code
Section 831-l-4 f b)
Dear Ladies and Gentlemen:
I have been reguested by two members of the Palo Alto
Planning Commission to submit to you for advice the guestj-on of
whether their respective interests in real property located within
300 feet of property that is the subject of a Planning Commission
decision (or series of decisions) constitute conflicts of interest
so as to disgualify them from participating in such decisions.
Background:
The Palo Alto Medical Foundation (rrPNvIFr') is proposing
to develop certain property it owns in the city of Palo Alto
(ttCityrr) to expand and consolidate its facilities. 
Approval of the
proposed development (the ttprojectrr) will reguire adoption of a
specific plan, amendrnent to the Cityts general plan and certain
rezoningT actions. In the City of Palo AIto, the Planning Commis-
sion (the "Commj-ssionrr) reviews all such proposals and makes its
recommendation to the City Council.
The Connission is curren+-Iy considering the proposed
specific plan and the Draft Environmental Impact Report (rrDEIRr')
for the proposed project. 
The Commission has closed the public
hearing on the DEfR, and the first of two reguired public hearings
on the specific plan. The Commission will be holding one or more
study sessions to discuss the specific plan and possibly to make
changes to it prior to holding the second public hearing. I woutd
estimate that the second public hearing will be held i; approxi-
mately one month, after which the Commission wilI send its pioposed
specific plan to the city Council. For purposes of the pofiticaf
Reform Act, the commission's action on the specific plan will
constitute a decision.
L,r)e


Fair Political Practices Commission
January l7, L99O
Page 2
RE: Government Code Section 83114(b)
The Phvsical Settincr:
The proposed specific plan covers a site to be developed
j-n three phases, within a rrcanpust area bounded by Ramona Street,
Channing Avenue, Waverly Street and Homer Avenue, plus a portion
of land across the street on Homer Avenue between Bryant Street and
Waverly Street. For your inforrnation, I have enclosed three pages
from the proposed specific plan to show the proposed phased
development. (Figures L6, L7 and 18.) I have also enclosed Figure
6 fron the proposed specific plan, rrhich sholrs the lotting pattern
of the area as well as the existing land use for all Iots in the
area. On Fiqure 6 I have marked the lots in which the tgro Planning
Cornrnissioners have an interest. 
You will note that commi-ssioner
Cullenr s townhouse development is 'tCatty corneredrr from the project
site and that a portion of the real property in which Cornmissioner
chandLer has an interest backs up to that part of the proposed
project which is across the street from the proposed campus.
Leqa] Analvsis:
I have written a letter to each cornmissioner advising
that I believe each has a conflict of interest and should not
participate, for the reasons set forth in the respective letters.
I have enclosed those letters for your further inforrnation.
The Planning Commission has asked that I request that you
use all deliberate speed in making your response, so that if either
or both of the Cornmissioners is found not to have a conflict, they
can participate in remaining proceedingTs. 1f you bel-ieve that your
response wiLl take nore than thirty days, f request that you advise
ne by tel-ephone of your tentative determination, so that I can pass
on that infonnation. If you need any further infornation to make
a determination, please 1et me know.
Thank ]iou for Your assistance.
Very truly yours,
Swc:jm
Encl osures
cc3 Mark Chandler
Patricia Cull-en
Ken Schreiber
Tony Bennetti
SUSAN CASE
Sr. Assistant City Attorney

RE: Conflict of Interest
Dear Pat: You and I have discussed the question of \"hether your
interest in certain real estate, namely, your residence, would
i"q"i." that you not participate in the r:ending decisions- affecting
tn6 pato Alt; MedicrL Foundation (PAMF). I have concluded that
your ownership interest would constitute a conflict of interest
ihat would require you not to so participate.
Facts
' 
You and your husband own a condominium at 23L Homer
street in palo Alto (the rtPropertyir ) which is located at the corner
of Homer and Ramona. The Property is across the street frorn the
site of the Proposed PAI'{F campus that is the subj ect of a proposed
Specific p1in. - For purposes of the Political Reforn Act (the
"ictr. generally, Titfe D of the Governnent code) your property
inier"=d is wiâ‚¬trin 3oo feet of another property 
- which is the
siUj ect of a proposed decision by the Planning Comrnission'
Leqal Discustions
The Act prohibits a public official fron participating
in decisions in which he knows or has reason to know he has a
financial interest. 
(Govrt code 5 87100) - 
nAn official has a
financial interest in a decision within the roeaning of Section
87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision !,,i11 have
a rnaterial effect, distingnrishabl,e from its effect on the public
generally, on the official ... or on any source of incoroe ...
aggreqating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by or proroised to the public official. within
12 months prior to the tirre when the decision is made.tr (Govrt
Code S 87103 (c) ) .
The Fair Political Practices Conrnission (IFPPC") has
pronulgated regulations to interpret whether a source of income to
a public official creates a conflict of interest in a particular
,'


Mrs. Patricia Cullen
January 10, 1990
Page 2
RE: Conflict of Interest
situation. 
These regrulations are set forth in Title 2 of
california Adninistrative code. In your particuJ.ar situation,
analysis is as follows:
cal.Adnn. Code S ]a7o2.3 (a) (1) states:
. 
rrThe effect of a decLsion is naterial as to real property
in whlch an official has a direct, indirect or beneficial ownership
interest (not including a leasehold interest), if ... the real
property in which the official has an interest, or any part of that
real prbperty, is located within a 3o0 foot radius of the boun-
dariel (or the proposed boundaries) of the property which is the
subj ect of the dLcision, unless the decision will have no financial
effect upon the officialsrs real property interest. r'
It appears that you do, indeed, have a confl.ict .which
would reguire you to disqualify yourself frorn participat-ing in the
current decisions concerning the proPosed development plan of the
PAI'IF.
As we discussed, I will subnit the question of your
confl,ict to the FPPC for a fomal ruling. You should nost likely
have an ans\,rer f rom then prior to the riext tiroe the Comrnission
considers the matter. In the meantine, however, I would advise you
to not participate in further proceedings.
As this conflict has only recently come to our atten-
tion, you have previously participated in the matter. However,
your pievious participatitn was tirnited to heariag a presentation
ir tU6 plan by ltatf anj listening to public testirnony. No action,
either tonceptual or final, has yet been taken. Therefore, no hArm
has been done.
I wil1 advise you further lrhen I receive the FPPC ruling.
sincerely,
pt^r-
SUSAN W. CASE
Sr. Assistant City Attorney

RE: Conflict of Interest
Dear l,{ark:
You inquired as to lrhether your interest in a certain
piece of real estlte would require that you not participate in the
lending decisions affecting the Palo Alto uedical Foundation
lnpnypil. 
I have concluded that your ovnership interest would
constitute a conflict of interest that would require you not to so
participate.
Facts
You have stated that you have an investment in a
partnership which owns an apartment building at 360 Forest-.in Palo
afto (the irBuilding"). The partnership owns- the Building and
improvements and leases the land. The. Building is adjacent to
property owned by the PAIIF and lies within the study area covered
Ly ite 
proposed- Specific P1an. For purPoseg- of the Political
n-eforn .tct- (Tit1e- 9 of the Governnent ccde'i the "4.t" 1 your
interest is within 3Oo feet of another proPerty which is the
subj ect of a proposed- decision by the Planning Commission.. :It
app6ars that the builcling itself is also rithin the boundaries of
tir-e specific Plan study irea, atthough that wilt not constitute a
confl,Lct of interest unless the proposed boundaries of the actual
Specific Plan site are changed during the process.
Leqal Discussion
The Political. Reform Act (the nActr; generally, Title 9
of the Governnent Code) prohibits a public official frorn par-
ticipating in decisions in which he knows or has reason to know he
has a financiaL interest. (Govrt Code S 87100). rrAn official has
a financial interest in a decision vithin the neaning of Section
87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision wiII have
a naterial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public
generally, on the official 
or on any source of income ...
aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or nore in value


I{r. Mark Chandler
January 10, 1990
Page 2
RE: conflict of fnterest
provided to, received by or pronised to the prrbl ic off-ici-al within
12 roonths prior to the tine when the decision is made. rr (Govrt
Code $ 87103 (c) ) .
The Fair Political Practices conflnission (rrPPcn) has
promulgated regulations to interpret whether a source of incone to
i public official creates a conflict of interest in a particular
sifuation. 
These regrulations are set forth in Title 2 of the
California Adninistrative Code. In your particular situation, the
analysis is as follows:
cal.Adm. code S La7o2-3 (a) (1) states:
'rThe effect of a decision is naterial as to real property
in which an official has a direct, indirect or beneficial ownership
interest (not i,ncluding a leasehold interest), if ... the real
property ii'r which the official has an interest, or any part of that
ieal prop"rty, is located within a 30o foot radius of the boun-
dariei (-or the proposed boundaries) o! the plgPerty which is the
subiect'of tn" alciiion, unLess the decision vri11 have no financial
eff6ct upon the officialsts real proPerty interest. tr
It appears that you do, indeed, have a. c-onfr.ict .which
would require you to disqualify yourself fron participating in the
current decisibns concerning the proposed developnent plan of the
PAMF. However, since your' interest in the real property to which
the Building is affixed is sonewhat unusual, I have taken the
precaution ot aaaing the following discussion. 
nn
For the purpose of this deterrnination, I have assurned
that the Building will 
come within '.-he definition of rrreal
propertyrr for purposes of the Act. 
The civil code (S 558)
definition of ireJl property" includes both land and buildings
thereon. The Act does not define rrreal Propertyrt. I acknowledge
that under certain arrangenents buildings can be separated froro the
Land on which they are located, for specific purposes, including
taxes. I do not believe that the Act makes such a distinction;
however, at this late date, I did not have a chance to confirn this
with the Fair Politica1 Practices Comrnission (trFPPcr). You have
indicated that your value in this Building bas increased in the
several years since you first invested, vhich seens to indicate
that its reLative value is tied to the value of the 1and. As such,
any potential effect in the value of the land lcould likewise affect
the value of the Building.
of ReaI Prape


.)
i,
(.
Mr. Mark Chandler
January 10, 1990
Page 3
RE: Conflict of Interest
If you would like me to submit this definition question
to the FPPC for a foraal ruling, I will do so. You would most
likely have an anstler from then prior to the next time the
Cornuriision considers this matter. In the meantime, however, I
would advise you to not participate in further Proceedings.
you mentioned that you had just remembered this ownership
interest, and wondered whether any previous participa-tion by
created ity problems, either for you or for the Commission. My
rt=porr=" i; itrat your previous participation has been limited to
t""ii1g a presentalion Lt tfre plin by staff and listening to public
testim5ny.- No action, either conceptuaL or final, has yet been
taken. Therefore, no hatm has been done.
If you wish to d.iscuss this matter further, or if you
wish to submit ttre guestion to the FPPC, please 1et me know.
Sincerely,

sections.question_synthetic: Do two Planning Commission members have financial conflicts of interest that require them to recuse themselves from decisions on a PAMF development project, based on their ownership interests in properties within 300 feet of the project site?
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, ocr; LLM: Document consists of three letters: (1) primary request letter from City Attorney to FPPC dated January 17, 1990; (2) letter to Patricia Cullen dated January 10, 1990 advising of conflict; (3) letter to Mark Chandler dated January 10, 1990 advising of conflict. The City Attorney has already made preliminary determinations of conflict but is requesting formal FPPC advice. Document ID 90059 indicates this is the incoming request that would have generated an FPPC advice letter response.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from the City Attorney of Palo Alto to the FPPC seeking advice on whether two Planning Commission members must recuse themselves due to conflicts of interest related to their real property ownership within 300 feet of a proposed PAMF campus development. The request includes background letters from the City Attorney to each commissioner advising them of likely conflicts under Government Code Section 87100 and FPPC regulations.
